Title: C# Case Sensitivity
QuestionText: C# is a case-sensitive programming language.

TrueFalsePayload.Explanation: In C#, 'Variable' and 'variable' are treated as two distinct identifiers.

Title: Reference Types
QuestionText: Which of the following are reference types in C#?

Title: String Mutability
QuestionText: Strings in C# are mutable, meaning they can be changed after they are created.

TrueFalsePayload.Explanation: Strings are immutable. Any operation that appears to modify a string actually creates a new string object.

Title: Access Modifiers
QuestionText: Which access modifier restricts access to the containing class or types derived from the containing class?

Title: Interface Instantiation
QuestionText: You can create a direct instance of an interface using the 'new' keyword.

TrueFalsePayload.Explanation: Interfaces cannot be instantiated directly. They must be implemented by a class or struct.

Title: Dependency Injection Lifetimes
QuestionText: In ASP.NET Core, which service lifetime creates a new instance every time it is requested?

Title: Async / Await Blocking
QuestionText: Using the 'await' keyword blocks the calling thread until the awaited task completes.

TrueFalsePayload.Explanation: 'await' yields control back to the calling method, allowing the thread to do other work without blocking.

Title: Garbage Collection Generations
QuestionText: How many generations does the .NET Garbage Collector use to group objects by their lifetime?

Title: LINQ Execution
QuestionText: Which of the following LINQ operators enforce immediate execution?

Title: Értéktípusok
QuestionText: A struct (struktúra) a C#-ban referenciatípusnak számít.

TrueFalsePayload.Explanation: A struct értéktípus (value type), ellentétben a class-szal, ami referenciatípus (reference type).
